channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: US Declares Power Emergency in Southeast as Heat Strains Grids
item.description: The Trump administration declared a power emergency in the southeastern US as forecasters warned of dangerous heat that’s likely to stress power grids along the country’s east coast.
item.dc:creator: Ari Natter

item.title: Gas Supply Woes Are Delaying Bangladesh’s Industrial Dreams
item.description: Inside a new Bangladeshi glass factory with a production floor nearly twice the size of a soccer field, machinery sits wrapped in tarpaulins. At a neighboring steel mill, construction has slowed and production has yet to begin.
item.dc:creator: Rajesh Kumar Singh, Arun Devnath

item.title: Why Your Summer Tomatoes Cost So Much
item.description: Prices are soaring after crop losses in Mexico and Florida, offering a glimpse of how climate disruptions could make food inflation longer lasting.
item.dc:creator: Emma Court

item.title: Scientists Urge Countries to Look Beyond CO2 to Tackle Warming
item.description: While carbon dioxide and methane are responsible for the majority of climate change, “indirect” greenhouse gases play a larger role than previously understood.
item.dc:creator: Eric Roston

item.title: El Niño Slams Into a Global Economy Unprepared for More Chaos
item.description: Scientists warn a blockbuster weather event risks pushing costs higher for everything from building materials to groceries.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Rosenthal

item.title: EDF Warns of French Nuclear Outages as Rhone River Heats Up
item.description: Electricite de France SA said it may be forced to curb nuclear output at the Saint-Alban nuclear site next week, a first sign that hotter weather risks tightening French power supplies this summer.
item.dc:creator: Eamon Farhat, Joe Wertz

item.title: A Rare ‘Super’ El Niño Is Looking More Likely. Here’s What to Expect
item.description: ​​A cyclical weather phenomenon that can drive flooding in one continent and drought in another has returned.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: California Heat Builds Ahead of Bay Area World Cup Match
item.description: High temperatures and gusty winds are elevating wildfire risk across Northern California through Thursday, leaving thousands of homes and businesses are without power.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: Newly Formed El Niño Likely to Be Among Strongest Ever, US Says
item.description: US scientists say the El Niño weather phenomenon that has emerged in the equatorial Pacific is on track to become one of the strongest on record, raising the odds of destructive droughts and floods in the months ahead.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan, Lauren Rosenthal

item.title: UK’s Growing Love for Paved Gardens Raises Flood Risk
item.description: There’s less vegetation to absorb excess water.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Rudgard, Alastair Marsh
